description: Create the react-frontend scaffold and complete initialization with Vite + React + TypeScript + Axios + Ant Design.
acceptance_criteria: Dependencies in react-frontend can be installed via npm install.
npm run dev starts successfully on port 5173.
The project includes the base directory structure (pages/components/services/styles).

description: Implement the main chat page layout aligned with the legacy structure: left control panel + right chat panel.
acceptance_criteria: The page includes model selection, network status, settings, privacy management, and chat area.
The input area and send button are usable, and the layout displays correctly on desktop and mobile.
No Gradio components are used.

description: Build a unified API client and configuration layer with support for local/cloud base URL switching.
acceptance_criteria: The Axios instance has unified timeout and baseline error handling.
Local/cloud configuration is managed centrally.
Frontend API calls do not use scattered hardcoded URLs.

description: Implement streaming chat requests and incremental rendering compatible with the /rag_chat data event format.
acceptance_criteria: Streaming content is appended incrementally to the latest assistant message.
The client recognizes content/info/error/done events.
Users receive readable error feedback when network interruption happens.

description: Implement auto/cloud/local mode routing and detection toggles (cache/network/complexity/privacy).
acceptance_criteria: Mode switching affects request routing strategy.
Detection toggles take effect in real time and participate in decisions.
In auto mode, routing can fall back to local or cloud according to strategy.

description: Implement an advanced settings panel (similarity threshold, retrieval count, complexity threshold) and link it to request parameters.
acceptance_criteria: The settings panel supports expand/collapse.
The three threshold parameters persist in the current session state.
Parameters are passed correctly to the backend when sending requests.

description: Implement a privacy management panel and privacy keyword management (add and refresh list).
acceptance_criteria: The UI can call /privacy/keywords/add to add keywords.
The UI can call /privacy/keywords to fetch and display keyword list.
Both success and failure states provide clear UI feedback.

description: Implement network status display and manual refresh.
acceptance_criteria: The UI displays basic network status and cloud API status.
The refresh button actively triggers status updates.
Error scenarios provide readable feedback.

description: Fix and implement the complexity detail modal: ensure model state synchronization and correct chat-history parsing.
acceptance_criteria: Current model state is consistent with UI selection and can be used in condition checks.
Chat-history parsing is compatible with the React message data structure.
The complexity detail modal can open, close, and render correctly.

description: Implement a response detail modal (basic stats, retrieval stats, document details) and provide an entry point to open it.
acceptance_criteria: The modal opens via a clear entry point.
Displayed content comes from streaming info events.
Closing behavior is normal and does not affect chat state.

description: Implement cloud-timeout fallback strategy and graded error messaging (auto-mode fallback, prompts for manual local/cloud modes).
acceptance_criteria: Timeouts in auto mode trigger a visible fallback.
Manual cloud mode failures do not force fallback and provide guidance.
Error messages do not expose irrelevant stack details to regular users.

description: Complete a pure-CSS style system while preserving functional information density and usability.
acceptance_criteria: Core pages are usable on both desktop and mobile.
Input area, message area, and sidebar do not block key actions on small screens.
Styles do not depend on Tailwind; only CSS files are used.

description: Establish a frontend regression checklist covering core chat flows and exception flows.
acceptance_criteria: Includes test items for auto/cloud/local modes.
Includes test items for privacy, complexity, network refresh, and streaming interruption.
The checklist is reusable and can be referenced in session records.

description: Add React frontend runbook and troubleshooting documentation, including explicit nvm + npm usage.
acceptance_criteria: Documentation includes nvm use 20 and npm install/npm run dev.
Documentation includes common errors and troubleshooting steps.
Documentation stays consistent with the actual directory structure.
